body:
### Problem

vim.net.request is limited to GET requests

### Expected behavior

_Moved from [discussion](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/discussions/38944)_


The idea for this plan is to add a new API to the `vim.net` module that mirrors the Python `requests` library, providing direct HTTP method functions (`vim.net.get`, `vim.net.post`, etc.) that all call a modified version of `vim.net.request` function.

## Current State

- `vim.net.request(url, opts, on_response)` only supports GET requests
- No body/post data support
- No HTTP method parameter
- Response only contains `body` field

## New API

### Signature Change

```
vim.net.request(method, url, opts, on_response)
```

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| method | string | Yes | HTTP method: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS |
| url | string | Yes | Target URL |
| opts | vim.net.request.Opts | No | Request options |
| on_response | fun(err?: string, response?: vim.net.request.Response) | No | Callback on completion |

For backwards compatibility we can have a local `request` function with the new signature and keep the current one marked as deprecated. All the new `vim.[verb]` methods will use the local function

### `vim.net.request.Opts` Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| body | string? | Request body to send (raw string) |
| json | table? | Request body as JSON (auto-serializes, sets Content-Type) |
| headers | table? | Custom headers to send |
| outpath | string? | File path to save response body |
| outbuf | integer? | Buffer to save response body |
| retry | integer? | Number of retries on transient failures |


### Response Fields

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| body | string | Response body content |
| status | integer | HTTP status code |
| headers | table | Response headers |

### HTTP Status Constants

```lua
vim.net.STATUS_OK = 200
vim.net.STATUS_CREATED = 201
vim.net.STATUS_NO_CONTENT = 204
vim.net.STATUS_MOVED_PERMANENTLY = 301
vim.net.STATUS_FOUND = 302
vim.net.STATUS_NOT_MODIFIED = 304
vim.net.STATUS_BAD_REQUEST = 400
vim.net.STATUS_UNAUTHORIZED = 401
vim.net.STATUS_FORBIDDEN = 403
vim.net.STATUS_NOT_FOUND = 404
vim.net.STATUS_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED = 405
vim.net.STATUS_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR = 500
vim.net.STATUS_BAD_GATEWAY = 502
vim.net.STATUS_SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE = 503
```

### Convenience Methods

All methods call `vim.net.request` under the hood:

```lua
vim.net.get(url, opts, on_response)       -- vim.net.request('GET', url, opts, on_response)
vim.net.post(url, opts, on_response)      -- vim.net.request('POST', url, opts, on_response)
vim.net.put(url, opts, on_response)      -- vim.net.request('PUT', url, opts, on_response)
vim.net.patch(url, opts, on_response)    -- vim.net.request('PATCH', url, opts, on_response)
vim.net.delete(url, opts, on_response)   -- vim.net.request('DELETE', url, opts, on_response)
vim.net.head(url, opts, on_response)     -- vim.net.request('HEAD', url, opts, on_response)
vim.net.options(url, opts, on_response)  -- vim.net.request('OPTIONS', url, opts, on_response)
```

## Implementation Notes

1. Auto-set `Content-Type: application/json` when `opts.json` is used
2. For `HEAD` requests, use curl's `-I` flag


Would love to hear some thoughts on this plan. I am able to start small draft implementations for each part if you guys think it's worth it

comment_data.body: > doing this as small incremental steps is welcome. we don't really have an "owner" for `vim.net` so doing too many big changes is less welcome, because it risks making painful mistakes.

title: vim.net as a full fledged http client

type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality
type.name: Enhancement
